HVAC CAPTURE02:14
MARK TEACHABLE MOMENT
NO-COOLAIRFLOW CUE
REVIEWED LESSONWarm return + frosted suction line
Check airflow before charge.
Novice trap: adding refrigerant first.
Safety: recover before opening the line.

ACT Capture for HVAC operators

Capture how your best techs think on real calls.

ACT turns senior technician ride-alongs into reviewed, company-specific training cards for new techs. The product happens after the job: capture, debrief, review, publish, measure.

Book a 60-day pilotDownload on theApp Store soonGet it onGoogle Play soon
Not a live diagnostic copilotLead-tech publish gateBuilt around callback reduction

Pilot loop

One field call becomes one reusable training object.

01Record

Senior tech captures a real diagnostic call.

02Mark

One tap when company-specific judgment shows up.

03Debrief

ACT asks the expert after the job, never during it.

04Review

Lead tech approves the moment and the lesson.

05Learn

Apprentices study the cue, reasoning, trap, and safety line.

06Measure

Outcomes tie training back to callbacks and ramp.

The training object

Generic catalogs teach the textbook. ACT captures the tribal layer.

Each card is anchored to a real job: what the expert noticed, what a newer tech would misread, what safety boundary matters, and how to check transfer with a quick quiz.

EXAMPLE CARD - TEACHABLE MOMENT 2:01

Frost on the suction line means airflow first, not charge

CALLBACK AVOIDED - COMPRESSOR PROTECTED
Observable cue

Warm return air, frost at suction line, blower sounds strained.

Expert reasoning

Low charge can frost too, but warm return points to starved airflow. Verify filter and static pressure before refrigerant.

Decision

Stop the charge path. Prove airflow first.

Verification

Check filter, blower wheel, static pressure, then re-check split.

Novice trapAdding refrigerant to a starved coil can overcharge the system and damage the compressor.
Safety boundaryRecover refrigerant before opening a line. Do not vent. Follow EPA 608.

Who uses it

Designed for the branch, not for a solo shop.

Senior tech

Records the job and explains the why in their own words.

Lead tech

Approves only the moments that match company practice.

Apprentice

Learns from reviewed cards built from real calls.

Ops director

Sees whether the library is moving callback and ramp signals.

Behind the scenes

AI agents do the paperwork. Your people keep the judgment.

Moment detectionMarks + transcript + frames surface candidate teaching moments.
Debrief questionOne sharp question captures the expert reasoning.
Training compilerTurns evidence and answers into a structured lesson card.
Safety reviewerFlags unsafe or unsupported claims before publish.
Ask ACTAnswers from published cards only, with citations.
Weekly reportSummarizes pilot output and operating signals.

60-day concierge pilot

Start with one HVAC branch, one senior tech, and real callback pain.

We help your team capture 20 jobs, publish 50 reviewed cards, and run a learner readout against the operating metrics you already track.
